title: Unable to sort or filter by mirrored columns
excerpt: It appears that you are not able to sort mirrored columns. Is this an issue for anyone else? I’m creating a project intake process that requires multiple mirrored boards and on of the steps will rely on a sorted mirrored…

posts.author: swilkbrooks (Stacey WilkBrooks)
posts.cookedHtml: It appears that you are not able to sort mirrored columns. Is this an issue for anyone else? I’m creating a project intake process that requires multiple mirrored boards and on of the steps will rely on a sorted mirrored column. Also, there is no way to filter by mirrored columns, even though they clearly show-up in both options.

posts.author: Margo (Margo Tsunemori)
posts.cookedHtml: I am also having the problem of being unable to sort mirrored columns. The drop down on the column shows that it is possible as does the hover on the top of the column. However, there is no sorting occurring.

posts.author: chrisdalynyc (Christopher Daly)
posts.cookedHtml: Yup - this started happening to me as well, I depend on mirrored columns in my tracking boards. Sort in mirrored columns was working fine until a few weeks ago.

posts.author: jmarjenka (Joshua Marjenka)
posts.cookedHtml: I’d also like to be able to show a column summary for mirrored columns.

posts.author: hdurey (Hunt Durey)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am able to sort and filter by a mirror column (mirroring a status column) BUT only when there is a single board in connect boards column. Once I add a second board to connect boards and mirror, I cannot sort the mirror column and the filters do not work. The mirror column does not show up in quick filters. Oddly, it does show in advanced filters as an option on column pick list, but when clicked, nothing happens.

I submitted this to Monday team a week ago for help. At least six different troubleshooters worked on the case asking for lots of info, video, console screenshot, etc. None of them seemed to be aware that this appears to be a platform-wide problem. They were focused on my specific boards and my web browser etc. They passed the case on to the developers Friday. I followed up suggesting this is a bigger problem than my specific case.

@AronK - as one of the leads on multi-board mirror roll-out, can you please update here on solution status for these problems with sort / filter on multi-board mirror columns? If helpful, my support request is #1680415. Maybe you can connect with the developers on this? This functionality is not useful without sort / filter, including in dashboards. I need this to work for org-wide annual work planning I’m rolling out right now with 25 staff, so this is urgent for us. Thanks!

posts.author: AronK (Aron-monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml: @hdurey appreciate the note and context! I was able to find that all of that troubleshooting enabled the team to submit this issue to be looked at by the developers. I do see that the support team has your ticket on hold which means that as soon as they are pinged from the developers, they’ll be able to circle back to you with an update.

posts.author: hdurey (Hunt Durey)
posts.cookedHtml:
Update from the Monday team and developers below. Sounds like it could be quite a while before a fix is implemented. For our team at least, this puts a big damper on the usefulness of multi-board mirror columns to roll up info from many boards and then filter for various purposes, especially in dashboards.

"Maddie here for the team! I’ve just received word from our developers.

After looking into this, they have realized that they will need to rewrite some of the filters code to support multi-board linkage. They are afraid that this is not a quick fix, and will take time to review and implement new code that supports multi-board linkage settings. With that being said, this issue has been moved to our bugs backlog queue.

While I’m unable to offer a timeline for when they will be implementing a fix, our developers will continue to consider it as they move forward in making platform improvements. Although it is considered to be of a lower priority than issues that severely impact platform functionality, we understand the impact this issue has on you and your team.

I recognize this is not ideal, but if there’s anything else at all that I can assist with in the meantime, please don’t hesitate to reach out. My team is here 24/7, and we’re always happy to help!"

posts.author: timlittletech (Tim Little)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi all, just pinging here again to bring this issue back up to light. Thanks @hdurey for submitting the details and info here. I’ve noticed that I’m also not able to sort by mirrored columns when I have 2 separate single board connection columns… Not sure if this is just my use-case. I’ll submit a ticket to monday support as this does reduce a lot of the usefulness of mirrored/connect board columns.

posts.author: PhilipC (Philip Chiappini)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m having this issue too. If it hasn’t been fixed has someone come up with a workaround/hack?

posts.author: cjmcqueen (Chris McQueen)
posts.cookedHtml: Any update for this issue? I just tried to filter on a mirrored column and was confused when the Advanced filters would let me select the column, but when I tried to select a condition or value it kept prompting me to select a column… very confusing.

posts.author: erusso (Elizabeth R.)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 on this feature request. This is a huge damper on our workflow and ease of use for our boards! We just created a high level board and are having that source all of our low level board common fields, like stage, CSM, contract dates etc. to avoid double entry. Finding out that we can’t sort the mirror columns is such a bummer!

posts.author: ColeG (Cole Gibson)
posts.cookedHtml: Jumping on this thread to keep updated! Would love to have this feature available for my team.

posts.author: Althaz (Dane Eife)
posts.cookedHtml: I too am joining this thread, apparently this used to work but no longer does and hasnt for almost a year? Is there any ETA when this will be implemented? It is very counter intuitive to mirror multiple boards into one but then not be able to filter through all that data.

posts.author: lki (Libby)
posts.cookedHtml: Adding to the chorus. Not being able to sort a column is bonkers.

posts.author: alexbb (Alex Espinosa)
posts.cookedHtml: Jumping in here as well. This is a much needed option to be able to sort mirror columns. I’m specifically needing to sort by dates using a mirror column timeline.

posts.author: pellegrino (Jeffrey C)
posts.cookedHtml: JFC can someone at Monday fix this!!!

posts.author: richradio (Richard Snell)
posts.cookedHtml: Man! This is terrible. Come on Monday!! not being able to sort using mirrored columns renders mirrored columns fairly useless in the context of reporting…

posts.author: Studio89 (Ralf Paul)

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)

posts.author: B-Rad (Brad)
posts.cookedHtml: Please let us know when this fixed. Need this as well.

posts.author: Holland (Holland Finley)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi all - I was also struggling with this and came up with a workaround. I created a new column that is a formula to reference the mirror column (e.g., New column = {mirror}) and then I could filter and sort off of the new column and hide the mirror one.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey everyone!

Would you be happy to elaborate further on the specific issues you’re running into with sorting/filtering mirrored columns (with more than 1 board connected)? We just want to ensure we are understanding the setback, so we can bring this forward with our product team!

Thanks

posts.author: oliviermarchesini (olivier Marchesini)
posts.cookedHtml: I am using a work around: I create a copy of the column I cannot use for filter (using formule) .

posts.author: Studio89 (Ralf Paul)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Bianca,

Glad to provide you with some details/live examples.

We have a View in place on an overview board, which is used to maintain an overall Job Status based on where individual subitems (“Subitems Team Job Status”) on five connected boards are currently at, and it would help to filter across the values coming in from these different boards (at least being able to filter on something like the defined “Done colors” because the wording on the Subitems Team Job Status is slightly different across the individual boards and may not be easily aligned due to business needs.).

Here is an example what currently happens when I attempt filtering:

When I go to the “Subitems Team Job Status” column and click on “Filter”, the Advanced filters open up and a new filter line is added at the bottom but the column selection in it remains empty.

Filter on Subitems Team Job Status

image951×330 29.1 KB

When then trying to manually select my column to filter on by name, I can find it but when I try to select it from the drop-down (circled in red), it just disappears.

Before

image1060×382 40.5 KB

After clicking on the selection in the drop-down menu

image951×330 29.1 KB

I hope this provides you with enough context on what we’re trying to accomplish and where we currently struggle with the existing system behavior.

On a related note: It may also be worth pointing out that the mouse-over information for statuses from connected boards does not accurately reflect the wording used on the specific board that the information is coming from. My guess is, that the mouse-over information is currently derived from the first connected board or something similar.

Best,
Ralf

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Ralf,

Thanks so much for sharing this information - really helpful. I have been able top test this on my end, and am not encountering the same issue:

Screen Shot 2022-12-16 at 11.13.17 am934×464 27.8 KB

Screen Shot 2022-12-16 at 11.10.51 am1204×440 30.5 KB

Screen Shot 2022-12-16 at 11.10.59 am1180×438 31.7 KB

As such, I am afraid this sounds like a potential bug Similarly, with the inaccurate wording for those mirrored status labels. So that our technical specialists can investigate your case, would you be happy to reach out to support@monday.com with this information? I apologise for the double work here Ralf!!

posts.author: Studio89 (Ralf Paul)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Bianca,

You’re welcome and thank you for doing some testing on your end as well.

We have quite a large amount of different statuses on the Team Boards and while some of them overlap in terms of wording, there could be a total of more than the 40 statuses I know are allowed per status column. Would it be worth you checking if you can reproduce the error by increasing the amount of Subitem Statuses on the Connected Boards?

In any case, my colleagues informed me that our company has already scheduled discussions on establishing an ongoing support process with you/the Monday team, so I won’t reach out to the support@monday.com for now but I will definitely make a note of this particular bug/behavior and raise it in those upcoming discussions.

posts.author: hdurey (Hunt Durey)
posts.cookedHtml:
@BiancaT We have a similar filtering problem: you can’t filter by a mirror of a mirror column. Example for 3 Boards:
A [Staff]
B [Projects]
C [Funding]
where Board A is connected to Board B and Board B is connected to Board C. Board B mirrors a column (Project Manager) from Board A, and Board C mirrors the mirrored Project Manager column from Board B. You can’t filter Board C by the double-mirrored Project Manager column. The advanced filter shows that column in the selection drop list, but when selected, it does not establish a filter.

This is a problem because we only want the Project Manager value to be designated on Board B [Projects] (via connection of items to Board A [Staff]) and then mirrored on whatever other boards are connected to Board B, like Board C [Funding]. That way, whenever the Project Manager value is selected on Board B [Projects], it shows correctly in mirrored columns across all other boards connected to Board B. But we need to be able to filter by Project Manager in those Board C double-mirrored columns. Hopefully that makes sense and you are able to replicate this behavior.

posts.author: gustavo.fialho (Gustavo Fialho)
posts.cookedHtml:
It’s a serious limitation. One of the main reasons to embrace Monday in my organization was its potential to integrate tasks from multiple boards. Mirroring seemed the perfect answer right from the beginning.

Honestly, I do understand a system is never perfect and should always be subject to improvement, but Monday Team’s approach so far is a shame. This thread has been running for over a year and we still don’t have proper response.

It’s a (highly) paid service. Get your stuff together, Monday.

posts.author: djb85 (Derek Byrne)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am now experiencing the very same issue as @Studio89

Is this a bug your developer team are aware of an working on a fix?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi everyone! I am sorry to hear of these issues, and completely understand how frustrating they can be. I followed up with our developers yesterday on this, and they confirmed they’re well aware of this issue, although are unable specify an ETA for a fix (I know this isn’t what you want to hear…) as they are currently combatting long-standing high severity bugs - however as mentioned this is certainly not a pain-point they are ignoring and plan for it solved in the near future. I really appreciate your understanding of this and of course, I will provide updates on this matter as soon as I am provided the information

posts.author: jesseallouf (Jesse Allouf)
posts.cookedHtml:
Also jumping in here @BiancaT - I have 12 people and growing on my marketing team. Every team member has their own board that rolls up into a central team board.

We decided to use mirrored columns to reduce duplicative work and allow maximum flexibility of working in any board they choose, knowing that each instance would update accordingly.

And now I’m trying to create filtered views by business line, since i have team members that focus on different businesses within my organization, and the filtering is not working. I can supply screenshots if needed, but it seems like the developers have this in queue.

I wanted to make sure I added to volume of concern here because I agree with everyone here that this use case is an important reason to use Monday and justify the cost to senior leadership, and if it’s unable to come through on the promise of streamlining workflows and making teams more efficient it is a concern.

Because then our teams won’t buy in to the use case, will not adopt the technology as a habitual action in there day to day, and another solution will be presented as an alternative to Monday that does have the functionality.

Thank you

posts.author: jesseallouf (Jesse Allouf)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you for suggesting this workaround. How does it work? I will try and play around to figure out in the meantime.

posts.author: Well (Well)

posts.author: graham.jose (Graham)
posts.cookedHtml: Adding a formula column is a workaround.

posts.author: rosalind (Rosalind Guy)
posts.cookedHtml:
Please reconfirm the formula.
We are trying to filter based on a country column that is mirrored

posts.author: Studio89 (Ralf Paul)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Rosalind,

Here’s the formula as I’ve been using it:

IF({MIRROR COLUMN NAME}=“Empty”,“”,{MIRROR COLUMN NAME})

Hope that’s helpful.

posts.author: rosalind (Rosalind Guy)
posts.cookedHtml: thank you that works perfectly.

posts.author: belliott (Ben Elliott)
posts.cookedHtml: Wow, it looks like this post has already had a lot of activity with little change. Our issue is exactly the one posted by @hdurey above (post #33 explaining that a mirror of a mirrored column cannot be filtered or sorted). An update would be appreciated. Is support doing anything about this, working toward a resolution, or is this still at the bottom of the priority list? I will say for all the issues we are having with Monday.com funtionality, this is by far #1.

posts.author: Mel_Schulien (Mel S.)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi! Waiting on this to be corrected as well! We have a lot of boards mirroring off each other but the fact that you can not sort is a huge issue for us!
Please advise!!
The formula is not working for me wen I am trying to use dates.

posts.author: lisaavila (Lisa Avila)
posts.cookedHtml: These discussion groups are hard to follow - I can’t tell if Monday is claiming that this works or knows that it doesn’t. I have a simple case of mirroring some columns on another board. The column that shows the connected item (which is of course text) can be sorted. Any mirrored columns that are numbers or text or status from that connected board can be sorted. However, if I have a column in that connected board that is showing a summary of a subitem column, then it cannot be sorted. If this is a known limitation, can Monday please update their documentation to admit this. And, can you please indicate if it will remain a missing feature into the future (because it is for some reason difficult to do), or if you will at some point add this feature? Currently, to get around this I create a formula that is simply equal to the mirrored column value, and I can sort on that (but that means I have to have two columns for every column I want to mirror - and there are limitations on what you can do with formula columns so that causes other issues).

posts.author: Kos (Kos)
posts.cookedHtml:
(Bumping this thread)

The problem with this workaround is that it is not possible to copy the contents from the formula cell. Which is needed often.

posts.author: MandarS (Mandar S.)
posts.cookedHtml: I guess with most tools nowadays I still miss these simple UX features. Understand now why Excel is still the king.

posts.author: KatBony (Katherine (Kat) Bony)
posts.cookedHtml: Only works for the initial mirrored information - if you mirror information from Board A to Board B, and then want to mirror that same information from Board B to Board C you can see it but can’t filter by it on Board C which defeats the purpose of not inputing the same information multiple times/multiple places.

posts.author: tim.sheaffer1 (Tim Sheaffer)
posts.cookedHtml: @BiancaT – Do you know if this is something that will be addressed in the near future?

posts.author: brad-explore (Brad Law)
posts.cookedHtml:
The Developer API references that you can’t filter on mirror columns. Mirror

I’m using Softr to connect with Monday and recently they removed the capability to filter on the columns even though it was working for me with the basic use case of a mirrored column of one connected board. They have since enabled that ability for me, but it’s questionable if they will continue to have that enabled.

posts.author: YSteinberg (Yoel S)
posts.cookedHtml: I can sort mirrored column on desktop but not mobile. Anyone have that?

posts.author: elliemrowe (Ellie)
posts.cookedHtml:
Just wondered if this is going to be resolved any time soon? It’s having heavy implications on our use of monday.CRM.

We have a Companies board which displays all of the current open projects associated with these companies through a two-way connect board column. I have then added a Conversion Rate mirror column displaying the stages of all of the currently associated jobs, and then a mirror column called Total Revenue.

‘Total Revenue’ is a mirror column that displays numbers that are also from mirrored columns. Total Revenue is a mirror column on our Jobs board which displays the total from invoices for each job. The information starts out on our invoices board, gets mirrored to the jobs board which in turn gets mirrored to the Companies board.

I’m not able to filter or sort the ‘Total Revenue’ Column on the Companies board and that means I can’t easily see the highest spend companies in descending order and this means we can’t have accurate or useful reporting.

It would be fantastic if this could be sorted out asap!? Or if anyone has a workaround I’d love to know.

posts.author: kirst (Kirst)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Bianca - As other’s have mentioned, the filter for mirror columns only appears to work if only one item is connected.

This is a problem because the filters still allow you to create the filter for the Mirrored Status column (with multiple items connected), with no indication to users that it only works when a single item is connected.

For one of our use cases, we have a Payments board that is connected to our Projects board, with a mirror column in the Projects board showing the status of each payment. One project will always have more than one payment. We have set up a filter to view all projects that have a “Payment Status” is not “All Done Labels”. We’ve been using this for weeks and it’s only been brought to our attention today that the filter is not working correctly.

Do you have any updates on this?

posts.author: calvin (Calvin Osborn)
posts.cookedHtml: The most effective way to address this is to create a column you can copy mirror columns to through automation in Make.com or create custom recipes in Monday.com. This worked for a project we were working on. Check out this LinkedIn post for more details.
